pre_handoff_head: 34945da archive old orchestration, add web-discovery skill
objective_for_next_orchestrator: Take the accepted Rust MVP autocomplete engine from local-source MVP to a better daily-use/developer-preview tool, starting with documentation correction, packaging, diagnostics, latency, and quality/privacy hardening.

current_mvp_status.summary: One local Rust broker serves VS Code and Obsidian over the shared protocol. Provider switching is broker-config-only. Real VS Code Extension Development Host and real disposable-vault Obsidian GUI smokes demonstrated visible ghost text and Tab acceptance. Bounded pi-backed smokes produced simple TypeScript and Markdown suggestions on synthetic contexts.
fresh_validation_from_acceptance: node --check clients/obsidian/main.js, clients/vscode/src/extension.js, and clients/obsidian/src/main.js passed
important_evidence: MVP-071: VS Code mock and bounded pi GUI smokes passed.
MVP-078: Obsidian mock GUI smoke passed with plugin load, broker POST, visible ghost text, and Tab note.md change.
MVP-078: Obsidian bounded pi request-only GUI smoke passed with visible synthetic Markdown ghost text.
MVP-074: final acceptance recheck cleared all blockers and listed only non-blocking limitations.

project_rules: Do not read tmp/, archive/, or unrelated directories under ~/dev unless the user explicitly references them.
Treat files tagged human-written as read-only unless the user explicitly asks to edit them.
For file-modifying sessions, run the checkpoint skill before ending and at coherent milestones.
user_orchestration_rules_from_previous_r: Use the pi worker through the orchestration skill for delegated worker tasks.
Do not use the subagent interface for ordinary worker tasks.
Use subagents only to debug pi-worker issues if the pi worker has a problem.
For implementer tasks, require the tdd skill.
Independent verifier workers must verify implementation tasks.
quality_gates_to_carry_forward: No platform client imports provider-specific code.
No autocomplete request enables pi tools.
No autocomplete request implicitly reads arbitrary files.
All provider outputs pass postprocessing before reaching clients.
All requests have deadline and cancellation handling.
Sensitive path exclusions are applied before remote provider calls.

known_pi_worker_behavior: The watcher log can remain empty until completion.

Several runs stalled inside pi before or after partial edits. If a worker is idle with no handoff/status/child commands for a long time, spawn a debug-only subagent to inspect process state, then either wait, stop, or requeue based on evidence.
For GUI or long-running tasks, require immediate in-progress handoff writes and explicit timeouts around every broker, npm, Obsidian, VS Code, Playwright/CDP, or pi command.
Do not trust partial edits from a stalled worker until validation and an independent verifier task are complete.

non_blocking_limitations_from_acceptance: pi-backed autocomplete latency is seconds-scale in recorded smokes; good enough for manual or idle-after-pause MVP, not Copilot-like per-keystroke behavior.
GUI smokes are bounded single-scenario validations on disposable synthetic files/notes, not broad daily-use acceptance across languages, vaults, keymaps, or extension/plugin conflicts.

Obsidian pi Tab acceptance was not repeated to avoid extra provider calls; Obsidian Tab acceptance is trusted from mock smoke in the same host, while pi request-only evidence proves real-provider ghost text visibility.
Marketplace packaging/public distribution remains outside the MVP scope; current evidence covers local-source/local-artifact setup.
stale_docs_to_fix_first: README.md current limitations still says GUI rendering/Tab acceptance are not covered by real editor smoke. That was true before MVP-071/MVP-078, but is now stale.

docs/limitations-and-next-steps.md still says real VS Code and Obsidian UI smoke has not been run and Obsidian module resolution is unproven. That is stale after MVP-071, MVP-075, MVP-077, and MVP-078.
Docs should distinguish accepted MVP evidence from non-blocking limitations rather than retaining old blocker language.

suggested_next_queue.title: Reconcile README and limitations docs with final acceptance
suggested_next_queue.scope: Update docs so setup and limitations reflect the accepted MVP and no longer claim real GUI smoke is missing.
goals: Use MVP-074, MVP-071, and MVP-078 as truth sources.
Update README.md and docs/limitations-and-next-steps.md.
Keep latency, packaging, quality, and scope limitations explicit.
Add a short 'ready for local-source use' statement and link to docs/client-setup.md.
success_criteria: No doc claims contradict MVP-074 final acceptance.
Docs still warn that pi latency is seconds-scale and packaging is not Marketplace-ready.
A verifier task independently checks the docs against MVP-074.

suggested_next_queue.title: Package VS Code client as an installable local VSIX
suggested_next_queue.scope: Remove repository-relative runtime dependency assumptions for VS Code and produce an installable artifact for local testing.
goals: Bundle or copy clients/protocol into the VS Code extension artifact.
Add package/build scripts and artifact freshness checks.
Add tests that load the VS Code extension from an installed/package-like layout, not only --extensionDevelopmentPath.
Document install, uninstall, and update steps.
success_criteria: A local VSIX can be built from a clean checkout.
An isolated VS Code Extension Development Host or installed-extension smoke loads the packaged extension and shows mock ghost text.
Provider selection remains broker-only and client boundary tests pass.

suggested_next_queue.title: Package Obsidian client as a local release artifact
suggested_next_queue.scope: Make Obsidian installation copyable without relying on repository symlinks.
goals: Add a release artifact command that emits manifest.json, main.js, and styles.css into a versioned directory or zip.
Keep clients/protocol bundled into main.js and host externals limited to Obsidian/CodeMirror runtime modules.
Add freshness/layout tests for the release artifact.
Document disposable-vault and real-vault install/update steps.
success_criteria: A copied artifact loads in a disposable vault.
Real Obsidian mock GUI smoke passes from the packaged artifact.
No repository files are written through vault plugin data during smoke.

suggested_next_queue.title: Improve broker and client diagnostics
suggested_next_queue.scope: Make failures observable without leaking sensitive context.
goals: Include configured pi provider namespace in /health and autocomplete response source.
Return or log privacy rejection, timeout, malformed provider output, and broker-down categories safely.
Expose minimal client-visible diagnostics in VS Code and Obsidian, such as status output or command/log channel, without dumping prompt/context.
success_criteria: Tests cover health/source strings with provider=openai-codex and model=gpt-5.5.
Client tests verify broker failure categories do not crash providers.
Privacy tests confirm sensitive context is not surfaced in diagnostics.

suggested_next_queue.title: Reduce provider latency path
suggested_next_queue.scope: Assess and design a lower-latency provider path behind the existing broker trait.
goals: Measure current broker overhead separately from pi subprocess/provider time over a larger synthetic fixture set.
Evaluate persistent pi worker/RPC options if available.
Evaluate direct provider APIs or local FIM providers behind the same Rust Provider trait.
Recommend one implementation path with risk, privacy, and subscription constraints.
success_criteria: Report median/p95 for current path over meaningful n.
Clear recommendation for NEXT-041 implementation task or no-go.
No client protocol changes required unless explicitly justified.

suggested_next_queue.title: Add quality and latency evaluation harness
suggested_next_queue.scope: Create repeatable code and Markdown autocomplete fixtures and metrics.
goals: Add fixture suites for TypeScript/Rust/Python/Markdown note completions.
Record acceptance checks, no-explanation constraints, max length, and latency buckets.
Separate broker overhead from provider time when possible.
Produce machine-readable reports for future regressions.
success_criteria: Harness can run mock provider quickly in CI/local tests.
Harness can run bounded pi provider manually with explicit opt-in and synthetic contexts only.
Results include median, p95, success rate, and common failure reasons.

suggested_next_queue.title: Harden privacy and richer context policy
suggested_next_queue.scope: Prepare for optional open-file or workspace-symbol context without implicit file reads.
goals: Add explicit local/remote provider state to config and diagnostics.
Add workspace allowlists and stronger default sensitive globs.
Design opt-in context providers with visible byte limits and audit metadata.
Keep default MVP behavior request-supplied only.
success_criteria: Tests prove remote provider calls do not read arbitrary files by default.
Opt-in context providers are rejected or redacted according to policy before provider execution.
Docs clearly describe what can leave the machine.

recommended_first_actions: Start with NEXT-001 before coding so user-facing docs match the accepted state.
Then choose either VS Code packaging (NEXT-010) if the user wants daily editor use, or diagnostics (NEXT-030) if debugging/observability is more important.
Keep latency work separate from packaging; seconds-scale pi latency is the main product-quality limit, but packaging is the main usability limit.
checkpoint_expectation: Checkpoint each coherent task slice: update daily-notes/YYYY-MM-DD.md and commit only relevant files. Leave unrelated dirty worktree entries untouched.
